content: We can reframe LLM self-reflection mechanisms by drawing on the language education definition of reflection as a structured learning activity, just like the guided reflective writing exercises taught in high school English curricula. This framing suggests that structured, scaffolded reflection prompts modeled after writing workshop activities can improve LLM self-reflection more effectively than unstructured self-evaluation, just as guided reflection helps students refine their writing better than unguided self-review.
